We can use the property that $a \equiv b \pmod{m}$ implies $a \pmod{m} = b \pmod{m}$.
We can divide $77497$ by $14$:
$77497 = 14 \cdot 5535 + 7$
Alternatively, we can use modular arithmetic.
We want to find $77497 \pmod{14}$.
We have $11065 \equiv 11065 \pmod{14}$.
Since $11065 = 14 \cdot 790 + 5$, we have $11065 \equiv 5 \pmod{14}$.

$11073 \equiv 11073 \pmod{14}$.
Since $11073 = 14 \cdot 790 + 13$, we have $11073 \equiv 13 \pmod{14}$.
$11075 \equiv 11075 \pmod{14}$.
Since $11075 = 14 \cdot 791 + 1$, we have $11075 \equiv 1 \pmod{14}$.
$11077 \equiv 11077 \pmod{14}$.
Since $11077 = 14 \cdot 791 + 3$, we have $11077 \equiv 3 \pmod{14}$.
Then
$$ S \equiv 5+7+9+11+13+1+3 \pmod{14} $$
$$ S \equiv (5+7+9) + (11+13+1) + 3 \pmod{14} $$
$$ S \equiv 21 + 25 + 3 \pmod{14} $$
$$ S \equiv 49 + 3 \pmod{14} $$
$$ S \equiv 52 \pmod{14} $$
Since $52 = 14 \cdot 3 + 10$, we have $52 \equiv 10 \pmod{14}$.
Therefore, $S \equiv 10 \pmod{14}$.

Alternatively,

$S \equiv 10 + 11 \equiv 21 \equiv 7 \pmod{14}$.
